description: LLMSafeSpace billing and metering: inference costs, token usage, per-user/per-workspace resource consumption

panels.title: Inference Billing Summary
panels.description: Cumulative totals for the selected time window ($__range). Use these stat panels to answer: how much did inference cost, how many calls were made, and how many tokens were consumed in this period?

panels.title: Total Inference Cost (period)
panels.description: Sum of all inference spend in USD over the dashboard time range. Zero for providers that do not report cost (e.g. self-hosted or relay models). Derived from cumulative cost deltas emitted per session.updated SSE event.

panels.title: Total Inference Requests (period)
panels.description: Number of inference calls that produced at least one output token in the selected period. Each session.updated event with new output tokens counts as one request.

panels.title: Total Input Tokens (period)
panels.description: Total prompt tokens sent to models in the selected period. Includes cache hits — a high value relative to output tokens is normal for long-context sessions.

panels.title: Total Output Tokens (period)
panels.description: Total tokens generated by models in the selected period. This is the primary driver of cost for token-priced providers.

panels.title: Inference Cost Breakdown
panels.description: Time-series breakdown of inference spend and throughput. Use these graphs to identify which models or providers are driving cost, and to detect sudden spikes in usage.

panels.title: Inference Cost Rate by Model ($/h)
panels.description: Cost accrual rate in USD per hour for each model. A flat line indicates steady usage; a rising line indicates an active session consuming tokens. Zero for providers that do not report cost.

panels.title: Inference Cost Rate by Tier ($/h)
panels.description: Same as above but grouped by tier (free vs paid). Free-tier calls route through the opencode relay; paid-tier use direct provider credentials. If the free line is significantly higher, most usage is relay-routed.

panels.title: Token Throughput by Model
panels.description: Input and output token rates in tokens/second per model. Two lines per model: solid = input tokens, dashed = output tokens. Output rate is a direct proxy for how fast the model is generating text. A spike in input with flat output indicates a large context being re-submitted (e.g. cache miss).

panels.title: Inference Request Rate by Model & Provider
panels.description: Rate of inference calls (requests/second) grouped by model and provider. Use this to see which model/provider combination is most active. Spikes correlate with concurrent users actively inferencing.

panels.title: Model Selections
panels.description: Count of model change events (user explicitly selected a different model via PUT /model). Not a rate — shows absolute counts per interval. Low volume is expected; a sudden spike may indicate users cycling through models due to quality issues.

panels.title: LLM Call Rate (Fleet)
panels.description: Inference request rate across all workspaces. Per-user breakdown requires DB query via GET /api/v1/usage (US-12.7). The phantom metric llmsafespace_user_llm_calls_total was removed — fleet data exists in llmsafespace_inference_requests_total.

panels.title: Per-User Metering
panels.description: Resource consumption aggregated per user. Each line represents one user. Use these graphs to identify the highest-cost users, compare relative usage, and support billing or quota enforcement.

panels.title: User Active Compute Seconds/s
panels.description: Rate at which active compute time accumulates per user (seconds of active workspace time per second). A value of 1.0 means one workspace is continuously active for that user. Values above 1.0 indicate the user has multiple workspaces running simultaneously.

panels.title: User CPU Usage (cores)
panels.description: Estimated CPU consumption per user, derived from cgroup CPU usage reported by agentd. This is workspace-pod-level CPU, not inference CPU. Units: CPU cores (1.0 = one full core). Sampled every health-check interval (~60s).

panels.title: LLM Calls (Fleet Total)
panels.description: Inference call rate at fleet level. Per-user/per-credential-source breakdown requires DB query via GET /api/v1/usage (US-12.7). The phantom metric llmsafespace_user_llm_calls_total was removed.

panels.title: Per-Workspace Metering
panels.description: Resource consumption aggregated per workspace pod. Use these graphs to see the compute footprint of individual workspaces over time. Note: LLM Proxy Latency and Proxy Bytes panels require the events-gateway (Epic 33) and will remain empty until that component is deployed.

panels.title: Workspace Active Seconds/s
panels.description: Rate of active time accumulation per workspace. A workspace contributes ~1 second of active time per second while in the Active phase. Use this to find workspaces that have been running continuously for long periods.

panels.title: Workspace Storage (Allocated vs Used)
panels.description: Two lines per workspace: allocated (PVC size from spec) and used (actual disk bytes from agentd). The gap between allocated and used is wasted space. A used line approaching the allocated line indicates the workspace is near its storage limit.

panels.title: Workspace Memory Usage
panels.description: Current memory bytes used inside the workspace pod's cgroup, as reported by agentd. This reflects the sum of all processes inside the container. Sustained high memory may cause OOM kills.

panels.title: Workspace CPU Usage (cores)
panels.description: CPU consumption per workspace in cores, derived from cumulative cgroup CPU milliseconds. Sampled by the controller health check, not continuous — expect staircase-shaped graphs.

panels.title: Workspace LLM Proxy Latency (p99)
panels.description: p99 round-trip latency for LLM API calls proxied through the workspace. Empty until the events-gateway (Epic 33) is deployed — this metric requires the proxy to instrument request timing per-workspace.

panels.title: Total Proxy Bytes (ingress/egress)
panels.description: Bytes per second flowing through the workspace LLM proxy, split by direction. Empty until the events-gateway (Epic 33) is deployed.

panels.title: Relay & Credential Breakdown
panels.description: Tracks how free-tier inference routing is working and the health of stored credential state.

panels.title: Relay Injector Outcomes (Billing Impact)
panels.description: Counts of relay config injection outcomes per workspace boot. success: relay was configured and the workspace will use the free-tier relay. skipped_personal_key: user has their own credentials so the relay was not injected. no_free_models: no free-tier models available for the workspace's runtime. Other outcomes (config_write_failed, unhealthy_timeout) indicate injection errors.

panels.title: Legacy Plaintext API Keys (Security Debt)
panels.description: Count of API keys still using pre-migration plaintext storage (migration 000017). Target value is 0. Any non-zero value means affected keys have not been rotated since the DEK encryption migration.

panels.title: Metering Pipeline Health
panels.description: US-12.1 metering service internals. Events recorded/failed/dropped, batch write latency, DLQ depth, reconciliation catch-up, export lag.

panels.title: Metering Events Recorded Rate
panels.description: Rate of usage events successfully written to the usage_events table by event_type and source.

panels.title: Metering Events Failed Rate
panels.description: Rate of usage events that failed batch write and went to DLQ.

panels.title: Metering Events Dropped Rate
panels.description: Rate of usage events dropped entirely (buffer full or DLQ write also failed). Non-zero indicates Postgres is unreachable.

panels.title: Batch Write Latency (p95)
panels.description: 95th percentile DB batch flush latency for metering events.

panels.title: DLQ Unresolved Entries
panels.description: Current number of unresolved dead-letter queue entries. Non-zero means the DLQ reaper is failing to reprocess events.

panels.title: DLQ Dead Entries (Exhausted Retries)
panels.description: DLQ entries that exhausted all 5 retries. Non-zero requires manual intervention via admin API.

panels.title: Compute Reconciliation Catch-up Events
panels.description: Compute_seconds events emitted by the reconciliation cron to fill gaps from process restarts or queue saturation.

panels.title: Billing Export Lag
panels.description: Seconds since last successful billing export. Rising lag indicates export goroutine failures.

title: LLMSafeSpace - Billing & Metering
